title: Remove the Idler Pulley Assembly
summary: Safely remove the idler pulley assembly from the tipper column so it can be replaced. The documented source procedure includes lockout/tagout, optional side panel removal for motor access, positioning and securing the A-axis and counterbalance before releasing belt tension, removing belt clamps, loosening the tensioning screws, removing the assembly mounting screws, and sliding the idler pulley assembly out of the column.
when_to_use: Use when the idler pulley assembly must be removed from the tipper column for replacement, as documented in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual section 7.3.20.1 Removal.
not_for: Do not use for installation or reassembly of the idler pulley assembly; those details are not provided in this source packet.
Do not use if the A-axis and counterbalance cannot be safely positioned and secured below both carriages before belt tension is released.
Do not use if the documented fasteners, holes, clamps, or screw locations cannot be positively identified from the source-backed equipment layout.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, gain access as needed, secure the A-axis and counterbalance, release belt tension, remove the idler pulley assembly, and transfer the tensioning screws if replacement requires it.

safety_notes: Perform the documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before beginning removal.
Secure the A-axis and counterbalance below both carriages before releasing belt tension to prevent dropping.
This procedure involves mechanical access, manual positioning of components, belt tension release, and hardware removal.
access_or_tools_needed: M6 socket-head screws for securing below both carriages
Access to the station side panel, motor area, actuator base, rails, belt clamps, and idler pulley assembly

access_required: Access to the station side panel if motor access is needed
Access to the actuator base, rails, belt clamps, and idler pulley assembly
system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out using the documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
A-axis and counterbalance can be manually positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
M6 socket-head screws are available to secure below both carriages before belt tension is released.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before accessing guarded areas and releasing belt tension.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure referenced by the manual before performing any removal work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in a locked out and tagged out state and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: Energy isolation is applied per the documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not fully locked out/tagged out or safe isolation cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure is unavailable.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure referenced by the manual.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if motor access is needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor area when required for the removal procedure.
steps.instruction: If needed to access the motor, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel, then remove the side panel.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed and the motor access area is exposed if needed.
steps.healthy_condition: Nine self-tapping fasteners are removed and the side panel is removed without obstructing access.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed, the fasteners cannot be identified, or motor access remains blocked.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Station side panel and motor access area referenced in nearby maintenance procedures.
Overall idler pulley assembly service context for this procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The station side panel or its nine self-tapping fasteners cannot be positively identified from source-backed equipment layout.
Motor access is needed but cannot be obtained safely.

source_evidence.text: If needed to access the motor, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel, then remove the side panel.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby text identifies removal of nine self-tapping screws from the side panel if needed to access the motor.
Removal steps instruct technicians to remove the station side panel if needed to access the motor.

steps.title: Position the A-axis and counterbalance between the actuator base holes
steps.purpose: Place the carriages in the documented position so they can be secured before belt tension is released.
steps.instruction: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base. There are two holes adjacent to each rail, and the carriages should be positioned between the holes on the respective rails.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis and counterbalance carriages are aligned between the threaded holes adjacent to each rail.
steps.healthy_condition: Both carriages are positioned between the holes on their respective rails and are ready to be secured.
steps.failure_condition: The A-axis or counterbalance cannot be positioned correctly between the holes.

actions.label: Position A-axis and counterbalance
actions.exact_action: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly service context and related A-axis drive area.
Actuator base, rails, and carriage positioning between threaded holes.
Similar A-axis and counterbalance positioning context below both carriages.

stop_or_escalate_if: The A-axis or counterbalance cannot be safely positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
The holes adjacent to each rail cannot be positively identified from source-backed equipment layout.

source_evidence.text: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base. There are two holes adjacent to each rail, and the carriages should be positioned between the holes on the respective rails.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Removal steps instruct technicians to position the A-axis and counterbalance between threaded holes in the actuator base before inserting M6 socket-head screws.
Removal steps instruct the technician to manually position the A-axis and counterbalance between threaded holes in the actuator base.
Removal steps include jogging the A-axis and counter-balance into position and inserting M6 socket-head screws below both carriages.

steps.title: Secure both carriages with M6 socket-head screws
steps.purpose: Prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping when belt tension is released.
steps.instruction: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.
steps.expected_result: Both carriages are mechanically secured below the carriages before belt tension is released.
steps.healthy_condition: M6 socket-head screws are installed below both carriages and the A-axis and counterbalance are restrained from dropping.
steps.failure_condition: One or both carriages cannot be secured, or the screw locations cannot be identified.

actions.label: Install securing screws
actions.exact_action: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages.

Holes below both carriages and carriage support arrangement.
Reference arrangement for inserting M6 socket-head screws below both carriages.

stop_or_escalate_if: The A-axis or counterbalance cannot be safely secured below both carriages before belt tension is released.
The holes below both carriages cannot be positively identified from source-backed equipment layout.

source_evidence.text: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Removal steps instruct technicians to insert M6 socket-head screws to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.
Removal steps instruct the technician to install M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping when belt tension is released.
Removal steps include inserting M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.

steps.title: Remove the belt clamps
steps.purpose: Free the carriages from the belt before releasing belt tension and removing the assembly.
steps.instruction: Remove the belt clamps by removing the six M6x20 countersunk screws from each clamp with a 5-mm hex wrench to free the carriages from the belt.
steps.expected_result: The belt clamps are removed and the carriages are free from the belt.
steps.healthy_condition: Six M6x20 countersunk screws are removed from each clamp and the clamps no longer retain the carriages to the belt.
steps.failure_condition: The belt clamps or screw locations cannot be identified, or the clamps cannot be removed.

actions.label: Remove belt clamp screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the six M6x20 countersunk screws from each clamp with a 5-mm hex wrench.

actions.label: Remove belt clamps
actions.exact_action: Remove the belt clamps to free the carriages from the belt.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly and related belt path service context.
Timing belt area and likely belt clamp location context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The belt clamps or the six M6x20 countersunk screws on each clamp cannot be positively identified from source-backed equipment layout.
The carriages cannot be freed from the belt after clamp removal.

source_evidence.text: Remove the belt clamps by removing the six M6x20 countersunk screws from each clamp with a 5-mm hex wrench to free the carriages from the belt.

steps.title: Loosen the tensioning screws to release belt tension
steps.purpose: Release belt tension so the idler pulley assembly can be removed.
steps.instruction: Release belt tension by loosening the two M6x35 socket-head screws on the bottom side of the assembly.
steps.expected_result: Belt tension is released from the assembly.
steps.healthy_condition: The two M6x35 socket-head screws are loosened and belt tension is released without the A-axis or counterbalance dropping.
steps.failure_condition: The bottom-side tensioning screws cannot be identified or loosened, or the secured components begin to shift or drop.

actions.label: Loosen tensioning screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the two M6x35 socket-head screws on the bottom side of the assembly.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly and bottom-side tensioning screw area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The A-axis and counterbalance are not secured below both carriages before belt tension is released.
The two M6x35 socket-head screws on the bottom side of the assembly cannot be positively identified.
Any component begins to drop or shift unexpectedly when tension is released.

source_evidence.text: Release belt tension by loosening the two M6x35 socket-head screws on the bottom side of the assembly.

steps.title: Remove the idler pulley assembly from the column
steps.purpose: Detach and extract the idler pulley assembly from its mounted position.
steps.instruction: Remove the two M8x25 socket-head screws holding the idler pulley assembly in place, then slide the idler pulley assembly out of the column.
steps.expected_result: The idler pulley assembly is removed from the column.
steps.healthy_condition: The two M8x25 socket-head screws are removed and the assembly slides out of the column as intended.
steps.failure_condition: The mounting screws cannot be removed, the assembly cannot be slid out, or the removal direction cannot be confirmed from the source-backed layout.

actions.label: Remove mounting screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the two M8x25 socket-head screws holding the idler pulley assembly in place.

actions.label: Slide assembly out
actions.exact_action: Slide the idler pulley assembly out of the column.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly mounting screw locations and assembly removal context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The two M8x25 socket-head screws holding the assembly in place cannot be positively identified.
The assembly does not slide out of the column after the mounting screws are removed.
The removal direction cannot be confirmed from the source-backed equipment layout.

source_evidence.text: Remove the two M8x25 socket-head screws holding the idler pulley assembly in place, then slide the idler pulley assembly out of the column.

steps.title: Transfer the tensioning screws if replacement requires it
steps.purpose: Preserve the tensioning hardware for use with the replacement idler pulley assembly when needed.
steps.instruction: If needed for replacement, transfer the M6x35 tensioning screws from the removed idler pulley assembly to the new assembly.
steps.expected_result: The M6x35 tensioning screws are available on the replacement assembly if required.
steps.healthy_condition: The M6x35 tensioning screws are transferred from the removed assembly to the new assembly when needed.
steps.failure_condition: The M6x35 tensioning screws cannot be removed, identified, or transferred as needed.

actions.label: Transfer tensioning screws
actions.exact_action: Transfer the M6x35 tensioning screws from the removed idler pulley assembly to the new assembly.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly context for identifying the M6x35 tensioning screws.

stop_or_escalate_if: The M6x35 tensioning screws cannot be positively identified on the removed assembly.
Replacement requires transfer but the screws cannot be removed or reused.

source_evidence.text: If needed for replacement, transfer the M6x35 tensioning screws from the removed idler pulley assembly to the new assembly.

success_criteria: The tipper was locked out/tagged out before removal work began.
The A-axis and counterbalance were positioned and secured below both carriages before belt tension was released.
The belt clamps were removed and the carriages were freed from the belt.
Belt tension was released by loosening the two M6x35 socket-head screws.
The two M8x25 socket-head screws were removed and the idler pulley assembly was slid out of the column.
If replacement required it, the M6x35 tensioning screws were transferred to the new assembly.
healthy_conditions: The tipper is safely isolated for maintenance.
The A-axis and counterbalance remain restrained after belt tension is released.
All documented fasteners, holes, clamps, and screw locations are positively identified.
The idler pulley assembly is removed without uncontrolled movement of supported components.
failure_conditions: The A-axis or counterbalance cannot be safely positioned and secured below both carriages before belt tension is released.
The documented fasteners, holes, clamps, or screw locations cannot be positively identified from the source-backed equipment layout.
The belt clamps, tensioning screws, or mounting screws cannot be removed as documented.
Any component begins to drop or shift unexpectedly during belt tension release or assembly removal.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the A-axis or counterbalance cannot be safely positioned and secured before belt tension is released.
Stop and escalate if the documented fasteners, holes, clamps, or screw locations cannot be positively identified from the source-backed equipment layout.
Stop and escalate if safe isolation cannot be confirmed through the documented Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
